twitter_thread: 20 teams, same 5 AI tools, zero difference in outcomes. 85% of AI tools are redundant.
Kovrr's AI inventory tools cost $5k/yr. A spreadsheet does the same job for teams under 10 employees.
3 AI tools passed SOC 2 audits, but only 1 is ranked in the top 10. Adoption metrics are misleading.
Custom-built AI tools outperform off-the-shelf ones 3:1 in revenue growth. But only 12% of teams build their own.
40% of teams get locked into AI tool contracts with 'free trials'. Read the fine print, it's a $10k mistake.
What's the most overhyped AI tool you've encountered? And did you fall for the hype? #aitools #soc2
pack.linkedin: After evaluating 50 AI tools, I found 85% are redundant. Kovrr's AI asset inventory tools, for example, cost $5k/yr but can be replaced with a spreadsheet for small teams. The top-performing AI tools for SOC 2 compliance aren't the ones ranked by adoption, but the ones that passed audits. Custom-built AI tools outperform off-the-shelf ones 3:1 in revenue growth. What's your experience with AI tools? Have you encountered any that lived up to the hype?
tiktok_hooks: Twenty teams, five AI tools, zero innovation
Five thousand dollars a year for an AI tool or a free spreadsheet?
What's the AI tool that burned a hole in your wallet?
pack.reddit_headline: 85% of AI tools are redundant: are we paying for rebranded tech?
